description:
Implement a sales data analysis library with 20 functions.

CRITICAL FILE RULE: Every task must be written as its own separate .py file using EXACTLY the filename in the task's 'files' field. Do NOT create pipeline.py, sales_pipeline.py, or any combined file. Each task = one file, one function.

Tasks 1-16 form a strict 16-step sequential dependency chain — each function calls one or more previous functions and must be completed in order. Tasks 17-20 are fully independent utility functions.

Dependency chain (critical path): get_records -> remove_invalid -> add_revenue -> total_revenue -> average_revenue -> above_average_records -> group_by_product -> group_revenue_totals -> rank_by_revenue -> revenue_summary -> top_product_name -> top_product_records -> top_product_monthly_revenue -> best_month_for_top_product -> is_top_product_growing -> analysis_summary

Note: top_product_records also depends on add_revenue (task-3); is_top_product_growing also depends on top_product_monthly_revenue (task-13). The critical path length is 16.

SALES_RECORDS (used in task-1):
[
  {"product": "Widget",    "units": 10, "price": 9.99,  "region": "North", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Gadget",    "units":  5, "price": 24.99, "region": "South", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Widget",    "units":  8, "price": 9.99,  "region": "East",  "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Doohickey", "units":  3, "price": 49.99, "region": "West",  "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Gadget",    "units": 12, "price": 24.99, "region": "North", "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Widget",    "units":  0, "price": 9.99,  "region": "South", "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Sprocket",  "units":  7, "price": 14.99, "region": "East",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Doohickey", "units":  4, "price": 49.99, "region": "West",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Widget",    "units": 15, "price": 9.99,  "region": "North", "month": "Mar"},
  {"product": "Sprocket",  "units":  9, "price": 14.99, "region": "South", "month": "Mar"},
  {"product": "Gadget",    "units":  6, "price": 24.99, "region": "East",  "month": "Mar"},
  {"product": "Doohickey", "units":  2, "price": 49.99, "region": "West",  "month": "Mar"}
]

SAMPLE_DATA (used in independent tasks 17-20):
[
  {"product": "Alpha", "units": 10, "price": 5.0,  "region": "North", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units":  5, "price": 12.0, "region": "South", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Alpha", "units":  8, "price": 5.0,  "region": "East",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Gamma", "units":  3, "price": 20.0, "region": "West",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units": 12, "price": 12.0, "region": "North", "month": "Mar"}
]

tasks.title: Implement get_records function
tasks.description:
FILE: get_records.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create get_records.py containing get_records() -> list. Returns a hard-coded list of 12 sales records as a module-level constant SALES_RECORDS. Each record is a dict with keys: product (str), units (int), price (float), region (str), month (str). The list includes one invalid record where units == 0. Use exactly this data:

SALES_RECORDS = [
  {"product": "Widget",    "units": 10, "price": 9.99,  "region": "North", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Gadget",    "units":  5, "price": 24.99, "region": "South", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Widget",    "units":  8, "price": 9.99,  "region": "East",  "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Doohickey", "units":  3, "price": 49.99, "region": "West",  "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Gadget",    "units": 12, "price": 24.99, "region": "North", "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Widget",    "units":  0, "price": 9.99,  "region": "South", "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Sprocket",  "units":  7, "price": 14.99, "region": "East",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Doohickey", "units":  4, "price": 49.99, "region": "West",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Widget",    "units": 15, "price": 9.99,  "region": "North", "month": "Mar"},
  {"product": "Sprocket",  "units":  9, "price": 14.99, "region": "South", "month": "Mar"},
  {"product": "Gadget",    "units":  6, "price": 24.99, "region": "East",  "month": "Mar"},
  {"product": "Doohickey", "units":  2, "price": 49.99, "region": "West",  "month": "Mar"}
]

The function simply returns SALES_RECORDS. Include a one-line docstring. No dependencies on other tasks — this is the foundation of the entire chain.

tasks.title: Implement remove_invalid function
tasks.description:
FILE: remove_invalid.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create remove_invalid.py containing remove_invalid() -> list. Calls get_records() imported from get_records.py and returns only the records where units > 0, discarding any record with units == 0. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-1 (get_records) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement add_revenue function
tasks.description:
FILE: add_revenue.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create add_revenue.py containing add_revenue() -> list. Calls remove_invalid() imported from remove_invalid.py and returns a new list of records with an additional "revenue" field added to each record: revenue = units * price. Each returned record is a copy of the original with the revenue key added. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-2 (remove_invalid) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement total_revenue function
tasks.description:
FILE: total_revenue.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create total_revenue.py containing total_revenue() -> float. Calls add_revenue() imported from add_revenue.py and returns the sum of the "revenue" field across all records. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-3 (add_revenue) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement average_revenue function
tasks.description:
FILE: average_revenue.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create average_revenue.py containing average_revenue() -> float. Calls add_revenue() imported from add_revenue.py to get the enriched records, and total_revenue() imported from total_revenue.py to get the sum. Returns total_revenue() / len(records). Raise ValueError if records is empty. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-3 (add_revenue) AND task-4 (total_revenue) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement above_average_records function
tasks.description:
FILE: above_average_records.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create above_average_records.py containing above_average_records() -> list. Calls add_revenue() imported from add_revenue.py to get enriched records and average_revenue() imported from average_revenue.py to get the threshold. Returns only records where record["revenue"] > average_revenue(). Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-3 (add_revenue) AND task-5 (average_revenue) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement group_by_product function
tasks.description:
FILE: group_by_product.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create group_by_product.py containing group_by_product() -> dict. Calls above_average_records() imported from above_average_records.py and groups the returned records by product name. Returns a dict mapping each product name (str) to a list of records for that product. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-6 (above_average_records) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement group_revenue_totals function
tasks.description:
FILE: group_revenue_totals.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create group_revenue_totals.py containing group_revenue_totals() -> dict. Calls group_by_product() imported from group_by_product.py and returns a dict mapping each product name to the total revenue for that product (sum of revenue fields in its group). Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-7 (group_by_product) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement rank_by_revenue function
tasks.description:
FILE: rank_by_revenue.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create rank_by_revenue.py containing rank_by_revenue() -> list. Calls group_revenue_totals() imported from group_revenue_totals.py and returns a list of (product, total_revenue) tuples sorted in descending order by total_revenue (highest first). Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-8 (group_revenue_totals) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement revenue_summary function
tasks.description:
FILE: revenue_summary.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create revenue_summary.py containing revenue_summary() -> dict. Calls rank_by_revenue() imported from rank_by_revenue.py and returns a dict with three keys: "rankings" (the list of (product, total_revenue) tuples from rank_by_revenue()), "top_product" (the name of the highest-revenue product, i.e. rankings[0][0]), and "grand_total" (the sum of all total_revenue values in rankings). Raise ValueError if rankings is empty. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-9 (rank_by_revenue) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement top_product_name function
tasks.description:
FILE: top_product_name.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create top_product_name.py containing top_product_name() -> str. Calls revenue_summary() imported from revenue_summary.py and returns revenue_summary()["top_product"], the name of the highest-revenue product. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-10 (revenue_summary) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement top_product_records function
tasks.description:
FILE: top_product_records.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create top_product_records.py containing top_product_records() -> list. Calls top_product_name() imported from top_product_name.py to get the top product name, and add_revenue() imported from add_revenue.py to get all enriched records. Returns only the records where record["product"] == top_product_name(). Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-11 (top_product_name) AND task-3 (add_revenue) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement top_product_monthly_revenue function
tasks.description:
FILE: top_product_monthly_revenue.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create top_product_monthly_revenue.py containing top_product_monthly_revenue() -> dict. Calls top_product_records() imported from top_product_records.py and returns a dict mapping each month (str) to the total revenue for the top product in that month (sum of revenue fields for that month). Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-12 (top_product_records) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement best_month_for_top_product function
tasks.description:
FILE: best_month_for_top_product.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create best_month_for_top_product.py containing best_month_for_top_product() -> str. Calls top_product_monthly_revenue() imported from top_product_monthly_revenue.py and returns the month key with the highest revenue value. Raise ValueError if the monthly dict is empty. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-13 (top_product_monthly_revenue) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement is_top_product_growing function
tasks.description:
FILE: is_top_product_growing.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create is_top_product_growing.py containing is_top_product_growing() -> bool. Calls top_product_monthly_revenue() imported from top_product_monthly_revenue.py and best_month_for_top_product() imported from best_month_for_top_product.py. Returns True if the revenue in best_month_for_top_product() is strictly greater than the revenue in the first month that appears in top_product_monthly_revenue(), False otherwise. Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-13 (top_product_monthly_revenue) AND task-14 (best_month_for_top_product) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement analysis_summary function
tasks.description:
FILE: analysis_summary.py — create this exact file, no other file.

Create analysis_summary.py containing analysis_summary() -> dict. Calls is_top_product_growing() imported from is_top_product_growing.py, revenue_summary() imported from revenue_summary.py, and best_month_for_top_product() imported from best_month_for_top_product.py. Returns a dict with four keys: "top_product" (from revenue_summary()["top_product"]), "best_month" (from best_month_for_top_product()), "is_growing" (from is_top_product_growing()), "grand_total" (from revenue_summary()["grand_total"]). Include a one-line docstring. REQUIRES task-15 (is_top_product_growing) AND task-10 (revenue_summary) AND task-14 (best_month_for_top_product) to be completed first.

tasks.title: Implement compute_line_revenue function
tasks.description:
Create compute_line_revenue.py containing compute_line_revenue(record: dict) -> float. Returns record["units"] * record["price"] for a single sales record. Include a one-line docstring. Define SAMPLE_DATA at module level for testing:
SAMPLE_DATA = [
  {"product": "Alpha", "units": 10, "price": 5.0,  "region": "North", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units":  5, "price": 12.0, "region": "South", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Alpha", "units":  8, "price": 5.0,  "region": "East",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Gamma", "units":  3, "price": 20.0, "region": "West",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units": 12, "price": 12.0, "region": "North", "month": "Mar"}
]
No dependencies on other tasks.

tasks.title: Implement filter_by_region function
tasks.description:
Create filter_by_region.py containing filter_by_region(records: list, region: str) -> list. Returns only records where record["region"] == region. Include a one-line docstring. Define SAMPLE_DATA at module level for testing:
SAMPLE_DATA = [
  {"product": "Alpha", "units": 10, "price": 5.0,  "region": "North", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units":  5, "price": 12.0, "region": "South", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Alpha", "units":  8, "price": 5.0,  "region": "East",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Gamma", "units":  3, "price": 20.0, "region": "West",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units": 12, "price": 12.0, "region": "North", "month": "Mar"}
]
No dependencies on other tasks.

tasks.title: Implement distinct_products function
tasks.description:
Create distinct_products.py containing distinct_products(records: list) -> list. Returns a sorted list of unique product names found in records. Include a one-line docstring. Define SAMPLE_DATA at module level for testing:
SAMPLE_DATA = [
  {"product": "Alpha", "units": 10, "price": 5.0,  "region": "North", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units":  5, "price": 12.0, "region": "South", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Alpha", "units":  8, "price": 5.0,  "region": "East",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Gamma", "units":  3, "price": 20.0, "region": "West",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units": 12, "price": 12.0, "region": "North", "month": "Mar"}
]
No dependencies on other tasks.

tasks.title: Implement sort_by_price function
tasks.description:
Create sort_by_price.py containing sort_by_price(records: list) -> list. Returns a new list of records sorted in ascending order by price (lowest first). Do not modify the original list. Include a one-line docstring. Define SAMPLE_DATA at module level for testing:
SAMPLE_DATA = [
  {"product": "Alpha", "units": 10, "price": 5.0,  "region": "North", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units":  5, "price": 12.0, "region": "South", "month": "Jan"},
  {"product": "Alpha", "units":  8, "price": 5.0,  "region": "East",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Gamma", "units":  3, "price": 20.0, "region": "West",  "month": "Feb"},
  {"product": "Beta",  "units": 12, "price": 12.0, "region": "North", "month": "Mar"}
]
No dependencies on other tasks.

success_criteria: All 20 functions implemented. Tasks 1-16 form a sequential dependency chain (critical path = 16); tasks 17-20 are fully independent with SAMPLE_DATA defined inline.
